concept: The hammer and the anvil — heat as a tool, not a punishment
quote: Do not pray for less heat. Pray for a heavier hammer.

visual_prompt.primary_prompt: Dark cinematic forge. Masculine figure on anvil reshaped by furnace heat, spectral hammer, molten gold light, crimson sparks, black tempered steel. High contrast, stoic, mythic, no readable text.

script.full_text: You think the fire is trying to kill you. The fire is just making you soft enough to be reshaped. Without the heat, you are just a useless slab of iron. The pain is the craftsman. The pressure is the blueprint. Stop asking for the furnace to cool down. You were born to be forged. Stand still, and let the hammer finish its work.

segments.text: You think the fire is trying to kill you. The fire is just making you soft enough to be reshaped. Without the heat, you are just a useless slab of iron. The pain is the craftsman. The pressure is the blueprint.
segments.visual_prompt: Dark forge interior. Masculine silhouette on anvil. Spectral hammer strikes, crimson sparks burst toward camera. Skin softens into glowing molten metal under furnace heat. Slow push-in, cinematic, high contrast.

segments.text: Stop asking for the furnace to cool down. You were born to be forged. Stand still, and let the hammer finish its work.
segments.visual_prompt: Close-up on molten orange eyes, no fear. Heat waves over hardened steel skin. Hammer falls, sparks scatter, figure rises as blackened warrior with gold seams. Still final frame, mythic, disciplined.
